title: Electricity (Class 10 Science)
introduction: This lesson introduces the fundamental concepts of electricity, including electric current, resistance, and circuits.
visual_description: Imagine a simple circuit with a battery, a bulb, and wires connecting them.

sections.heading: Electric Current
sections.content: Current is the flow of electric charge through a conductor. It is measured in amperes (A).

sections.heading: Resistance
sections.content: Resistance is the opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of electric current. It is measured in ohms (Ω).

sections.heading: Electric Circuits
sections.content: A complete path for electric current through which energy can flow is called an electric circuit.

activity: Students can create a simple circuit using a battery, bulb, and wires to observe the effect of increasing or decreasing the number of connections (resistance) on the brightness of the bulb.
summary: Understanding electricity is essential for understanding various modern devices. This lesson provides a foundation for further study in this area.
key_facts: Electric current is the flow of electric charge.
Resistance is the opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of electric current.
A complete path for electric current through which energy can flow is called an electric circuit.
